title: Divide whole numbers by decimals
description: Dividing whole number by tenths or hundredths like 8÷0.4.

hints: There are many ways to solve this problem. Let's see two possible strategies.

###Place value strategy

We can think in terms of tenths:

$\begin{align}
8 \div 0.4&= 8.0\div 0.4\\\\
&=80\text{ tenths} \div 4\text{ tenths}\\\\
&=20
\end{align}$

###Fraction strategy

We can convert the division problem to a fraction, then multiply the top and bottom of the fraction by $10$ so we can work with whole numbers:

$\begin{align}
8 \div 0.4 &= \dfrac{8}{0.4}\\\\
&= \dfrac{8 \times 10}{0.4 \times 10}\\\\
&= \dfrac{80}{4}\\\\
&= 20 
\end{align}$

###The answer

$8 \div 0.4 = 20$
There are many ways to solve this problem. Let's see two possible strategies.

###Place value strategy

We can think in terms of tenths:

$\begin{align}
6 \div 0.3&= 6.0\div 0.3\\\\
&=60\text{ tenths} \div 3\text{ tenths}\\\\
&=20
\end{align}$

###Fraction strategy

We can convert the division problem to a fraction, then multiply the top and bottom of the fraction by $10$ so we can work with whole numbers:

$\begin{align}
6 \div 0.3 &= \dfrac{6}{0.3}\\\\
&= \dfrac{6 \times  10}{0.3 \times 10}\\\\
&= \dfrac{60}{3}\\\\
&= 20 
\end{align}$

###The answer

$20 = 6 \div 0.3$
There are many ways to solve this problem. Let's see two possible strategies.
